author: Andrew Gallant
message: ignore-0.4.28

author: Amaan Qureshi
message:
ignore: support `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` and `GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM` for `core.excludesFile`

`gitconfig_excludes_path()` resolves the global gitignore
by reading `core.excludesFile` from `$HOME/.gitconfig` and
`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/git/config`, but git 2.32 (June 2021) introduced
`GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` and `GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM` environment variables that
override these locations. The `ignore` crate doesn't check either of
them, so users who point `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` at a non-standard path
(e.g. NixOS managing git config at `/etc/git/config`) have their global
gitignore silently ignored by ripgrep while `git` itself respects it.

This commit checks `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` before the hardcoded paths,
matching git's semantics where it replaces both `$HOME/.gitconfig` and
the XDG config. It also adds system-level config (`GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM`,
falling back to `/etc/gitconfig`) as a lowest-priority source for
`core.excludesFile`, which the crate previously didn't consult at all.

Fixes #3275, Closes #3276

author: howmanysmall
message:
searcher: hint at sequential memory map reading

Note that we continue to disable memory maps on macOS.

Note also that I've generally been unable to observe a performance
improvement from a change like this. However, I think it's fine to do
this for reasons of "good sense."

Closes #3246

author: Thayne McCombs
message:
globset: fix `matches_all` false positives

Make sure that we correctly check that all globs are matched, even if
multiple globs use the same strategy.

Previously we only checked that all strategies matched, but didn't
check with the strategies themselves.

This now adds a `matches_all` method to each strategy to check all
globs within a strategy.

This avoids allocating for tracking the number of matches.

Fixes #3290, Closes #3302

author: Andrew Gallant
message: changelog: bring unreleased changes up to date

author: Yujiang Wang
message:
ignore: fetch directory entry metadata in batches

On Windows probing the existence of each ignore file is
very heavy. Windows prefers fetching metadata in batches
(FindFirstFileExW/FindNextFileW, which translates to
std::sys::fs::read_dir)

Now, in ignore::Work::read_dir, we read the entire directory at once,
then use the result to determine the existense of ignore files. The
result is saved and visited later. This avoids stat'ing file paths that
don't exist.

Note that this also helps with Linux workloads too. For example, on
the <https://github.com/NightMachinary/r_HPfanfiction> repository:

```
$ hyperfine "rg-base 'with regexes,'" "rg-test 'with regexes,'"
Benchmark 1: rg-base 'with regexes,'
  Time (mean ± σ):     483.1 ms ±   6.4 ms    [User: 2493.1 ms, System: 3192.9 ms]
  Range (min … max):   474.8 ms … 496.0 ms    10 runs

Benchmark 2: rg-test 'with regexes,'
  Time (mean ± σ):     350.4 ms ±   5.2 ms    [User: 1652.6 ms, System: 2324.5 ms]
  Range (min … max):   341.6 ms … 358.5 ms    10 runs

Summary
  rg-test 'with regexes,' ran
    1.38 ± 0.03 times faster than rg-base 'with regexes,'
```

Fixes #3293, Closes #3410

author: Nipan Das
message:
ignore: add multi-root parent matcher regression tests

Parent ignore matchers are cached by directory across search roots, but
each root must keep its own absolute_base for path rewriting when applying
parent .gitignore / .rgignore rules. Document that invariant and lock it
in with unit and integration tests for the order-dependent multi-root
bugs in #3376, #3419, and #3320.

Closes #3320, Closes #3376, Ref #3419, Closes #3451

it was pointed out to me that i've been using -q a bit too loosely. this

author: dana
message:
test-complete: misc. improvements

- eliminate no_function_argzero. was only needed for old versions of zsh
  that lacked ZSH_ARGZERO

- fix misc. style/convention issues

- improve error messages

- eliminate redundant `diff --label` condition. the gnu diff
  documentation implies that it doesn't support -L, but it does

- test hidden ('!') arg specs. i may have been worried that hidden
  options wouldn't be mentioned in the help output, but this isn't
  actually a problem atm. we can re-assess if it becomes one in the
  future

available since zsh 5.9 (2022)

message:
_rg: improve existing type functions

- simplify
- fix encodings with a colon in the name

author: dana
message:
_rg: improve colour- and type-spec completion

- move to separate functions. we're already using type functions for the
  other actions so we might as well do it for these. it makes the main
  function a lot cleaner

- improve weird colour-spec conditions

- don't suffix 'none' attribute with colon

- improve completion of second part of type spec. (i don't think my
  comment about it being complex was true, i was either ignorant or
  unimaginative)

- general clean-up etc

message: _rg: update $no pattern to account for --require-git and --unicode

author: dana
message:
_rg: eliminate superfluous group comments

these weren't really necessary, it's self-evident what the groups are
for. rather than add more superfluous comments for the groups added in
my previous commit, we can just get rid of them

author: dana
message:
_rg: misc. spec fixes

- eliminate redundant groups
- eliminate redundant option specs
- use conventional description wording, clarify some descriptions
- complete --pre-glob argument
- offer files for glob patterns (why not)

author: dana
message:
_rg: add missing groups

i think these options didn't have their own groups because originally
there weren't --no-* variants for them. now there are, and they should
be mutually exclusive

author: dana
message:
_rg: remove group-stripping functionality

when this function was first written, the _arguments grouping feature
(added in zsh 5.4) was new enough that compatibility with older shells
was a real concern. but it's been almost a decade (!) since then so i
think it's fine to just assume we have it now
